cards.prompt: A throw in C (3 frames down) unwinds the stack through B (no catch) and is caught in A; the CLR executes the first catch block it finds, and e.Message is preserved as boom
cards.code: using System; string log = ""; void C(){ throw new InvalidOperationException("boom"); } void B(){ C(); } void A(){ B(); } try { A(); } catch (InvalidOperationException e) { log = e.Message; } Console.WriteLine(log);

cards.prompt: The inner exception (inner) is wrapped in an outer one (outer) via the second constructor argument, which stores the cause in InnerException; e.Message is outer and e.InnerException.Message is inner
cards.code: using System; try { try { throw new InvalidOperationException("inner"); } catch (Exception ex) { throw new ApplicationException("outer", ex); } } catch (Exception e) { Console.WriteLine($"{e.Message}|{e.InnerException?.Message}"); }

cards.prompt: Indexing out of range throws IndexOutOfRangeException; a base catch (SystemException) still catches it because all exceptions derive from System.Exception, but GetType().Name returns the real derived type
cards.code: using System; string r; try { int[] a = new int[2]; Console.WriteLine(a[5]); r="none"; } catch (SystemException e) { r = e.GetType().Name; } Console.WriteLine(r);
